pi_items.source: setup_task.sh and task.json description explicit requirements.
setup_task.sh and task.json description explicit requirements.
export_result.sh script explicitly parsing this path and task.json description.
export_result.sh script explicitly checking this path and task.json description.
Python rasterio analysis confirms landsat.tif has 4 bands and dem.tif has 1 band. Collocating them yields 5 bands total.
export_result.sh explicitly checks for a band named 'elevation_resampled' in the output DIM file, and it is instructed in the task.json.

Python rasterio analysis of the master grid (2127x1564 pixels) shows that 4 uint8 bands and 1 float64 band will produce an uncompressed TIF file of approximately 40 MB, satisfying the > 5000000 bytes check.

privileged_info_summary: The task relies on real Landsat and SRTM DEM sample data from the opengeos GitHub repository. The master file (Landsat) contains 4 bands, while the slave file (DEM) contains 1 band, meaning the collocated output should strictly have 5 bands. The output paths for the exported DIM and TIF files are explicitly dictated as /home/ga/snap_projects/ml_datacube.dim and /home/ga/snap_exports/ml_datacube.tif, and the task requires exactly renaming one of the bands to 'elevation_resampled'.
